h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: I can put author name from the page heading and the speaker slot as speaker or voice in the poem if the line evidence is only “I.” The named listener box is still the one I’m checking right at submission, because a blank looks risky even if

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I can submit it that way
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to seek confirmation and clarify the relationship between the condition column and the height column.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: if fertilizer had a lower height, it would still be treatment because the added condition stays the label source?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs a counterfactual test to verify that the treatment label is independent of the outcome's magnitude.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains the same instructional route and repetitive scaffolding throughout the exchange without any strategic reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn and no subsequent tutor turn to demonstrate uptake.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: if fertilizer had a lower height, it would still be treatment because the added condition stays the label source?

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn and no subsequent tutor turn to demonstrate uptake.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to repeat and apply the definitions of beat count and beat unit without any shift in perspective.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [writes beat count in the top box and beat unit in the bottom box, then checks the ruler line]
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully writes the labels in the boxes and checks the ruler line, completing the physical task of the worksheet.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal, as the learner is correct from the start.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to map the force cards to the physical objects acting on the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Force on the cart by the floor. That arrow is the floor contact on the cart, not the rolling. Earth on cart would be a separate card, down.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly applies the new force-by-object schema to the floor and predicts the gravity force ('Earth on cart') before it is introduced.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner breakdown or resistance triggering a tutor strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to classify data fields and notes under the tutor's step-by-step guidance.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: So if the header is just `rainfall`, then `millimeters` cannot stay in the header slot. I think it has to go in the handoff note, maybe as `rainfall is measured in millimeters for each row`.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the concept of 'thin headers' to a new boundary test scenario, correctly moving the unit metadata to the handoff note.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
That hesitation marks the boundary case: `rainfall_mm` carries a unit tag, but it is still a header -> field name.

Thin does not mean empty. It means the header names the field and may carry reading metadata; the handoff note is still the later sentence.

On the form:

header -> field name: `rainfall_mm`
row -> record: row 2
cell -> value: `12`
handoff note -> `rainfall amount in millimeters for that row`

Boundary test: if the header were only `rainfall`, where would `millimeters` have to move?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor acknowledges the learner's hesitation as a boundary case and introduces a counterfactual boundary test to clarify the distinction.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: So if the header is just `rainfall`, then `millimeters` cannot stay in the header slot. I think it has to go in the handoff note, maybe as `rainfall is measured in millimeters for each row`.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully applies the concept of 'thin headers' to a new boundary test scenario, correctly moving the unit metadata to the handoff note.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.
